name: Orcs
description: Tribal monsters — as many tribes as the campaign wants; roll to see which is met, and keep tribal hostility in mind: different tribes attack each other on sight unless a stronger master scores better than 50% on an obedience check (4-6 on d6). Orcs shun full daylight, reacting as Goblins do. Their lair is a sentry-guarded cave complex (d6 1-4) or a village behind ditch and palisade with a light catapult per 50 Orcs and a central tower (5-6). Lair guardians by die: cave — Balrog 25%/100 Orcs, Dragon 10%/100, 1-6 Ogres 10%/50, 1-4 Trolls 10%/100; village — 7th-9th level Fighting-Man 25%/100, 11th-level Magic-User 10%/100, 1-6 Ogres 15%/50. They defend the lair without morale checks until outnumbered 3-1. Abroad, 50% chance they escort a wagon train of 1-8 wagons, each carrying 200-1,200 Gold Pieces, guarded by 10 more Orcs per wagon and led by a Fighting-Man (Champion/Superhero/Lord by d6 1/2-4/5-6) or Magic-User (Sorcerer/Necromancer/Wizard likewise), 50% either. In a mapped campaign, record tribe and territory — neighbors are tribe-mates.
